title: Lycopodioides nicaraguensis
taxonomy: Lycopodioides nicaraguensis is a species of club moss in the family Lycopodiaceae. It is a vascular plant, belonging to the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a group of ancient plants. The genus Lycopodioides is part of the class Lycopodiopsida, and the family Lycopodiaceae is part of the division Lycopodiophyta. This classification places Lycopodioides nicaraguensis within the broader group of vascular plants that have a distinct evolutionary history. The specific classification of Lycopodioides nicaraguensis reflects its unique characteristics and its place in the plant kingdom.

intro: Lycopodioides nicaraguensis, also known as Nicaraguan club moss, is a small, perennial plant belonging to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae). Traditionally, Lycopodioides nicaraguensis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include lignans, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lycopodioides nicaraguensis is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies for its purported health benefits.
